$comment: Legacy-stack-class -> {blueprint, transform, validate, status} dispatch truth, read by the `modernize` archetype's `discover` phase (scripts/crew/stack_registry.py). The `status` field is the load-bearing honesty contract: this registry NEVER fabricates a migration for `planned`/`none`/unknown stacks — the reader emits a capability-gap task and STOPS, matching garden's fail-closed ETHOS ('we do not silently degrade ... never invent a pass'). The `planned` rows double as the modernization roadmap (each is a backlog item, not a capability). `blueprint`/`transform`/`validate` name the GENERAL code-modernization assets/skills that handle the class; this registry is stack-agnostic content, not an engine. status: wired = transform+validate exist and run; planned = on the roadmap, gap-task emitted; none = out of scope, gap-task emitted.

$status_levels.wired: blueprint + transform + validate all resolvable and runnable now. Dispatch proceeds.
$status_levels.planned: on the modernization roadmap but not yet runnable. Reader emits a capability-gap task and stops — never fabricates.
$status_levels.none: explicitly out of scope. Reader emits a capability-gap task and stops.

node-legacy-to-modern.label: Legacy Node/JS (callbacks, CommonJS, old framework) -> modern TS/ESM

transform.planned: AngularJS->Angular component/codemod wave not yet curated as a wired transform

validate.planned: differential-equivalence harness for the AngularJS->Angular boundary not yet wired

transform.planned: COBOL->Java rule-extraction + transform pipeline not yet wired as a runnable transform

validate.planned: record/replay parity harness for batch outputs not yet wired

transform.planned: .NET Framework->Core upgrade-assistant-class transform not yet wired

validate.planned: parity harness for the .NET boundary not yet wired
